title: Overview
points: Vertical farming in Jordan is an innovative agricultural practice that involves growing crops in vertically stacked layers, often integrated into controlled environments. This method maximizes space usage, conserves water, and enables year-round production, making it ideal for Jordan's arid climate and limited arable land.

title: Benefits
points: Efficient use of space in urban areas
Up to 90% less water usage compared to traditional farming
Reduced transportation costs and carbon footprint
Consistent crop yields regardless of season

title: Challenges
points: Energy requirements for lighting and climate control

title: Key Crops
points: Lettuce, spinach, herbs (mint, basil), strawberries, tomatoes, cucumbers.

title: Notable Projects
points: Amman Urban Farm: A pilot vertical farm in the capital city.
Aqaba Green Towers: Hydroponic vertical farming for leafy greens.
University of Jordan Research Facility: Academic research and demonstration site.
